title: Aureline private-triage workspace packet
description: Boundary schema for the private_triage_workspace_packet_record frozen in docs/security/intake_and_triage.md. One record per triage carries the operational state the monitored SECURITY.md contact path keeps while a report moves through intake, triage, mitigation, disclosure, and postmortem. The packet pairs 1:1 with an incident_workspace_packet_record (evidence-capture companion: timeline, evidence-embedding, continuity) and 1:N with advisory_record entries; advisory-ID aliases, affected-build identity, affected-artifact identity, install-profile cards, and deployment-profile scope are shared by identity rather than adapter-specific aliases. The record carries intake_received_at, intake_acknowledged_at, current_mitigation_status, compensating_control_state, break_glass_state, disclosure_posture, redaction_posture, advisory_alias_reservation, linked_advisory_refs, linked_postmortem_refs, and linked_emergency_action_refs / linked_revocation_refs, plus the monitored_contact_ref every report resolves against. Raw binary bytes, raw signing material, raw exploit payloads, raw reporter identities, and raw secret material never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and redaction-class metadata only. Adding a new triage_status_class, current_mitigation_status_class, compensating_control_state_class, break_glass_state_class, disclosure_posture_class, or denial-reason value is additive-minor and requires a private_triage_workspace_packet_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row co-signed by security_trust_review and release_council.

private_triage_workspace_packet_schema_v.description: Integer schema version for this private-triage workspace packet export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator. Every packet carries record_kind = private_triage_workspace_packet_record so parity audits and support exports can validate payloads mechanically.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / release-evidence packets. Raw binary bytes, raw signing material, raw dependency source, raw evidence bodies, and raw reporter identities never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short, reviewable sentence describing a posture, note, or observation. Raw secret material and raw exploit payloads MUST NOT appear.

triage_status_class.description: Frozen triage-status vocabulary. Mirrors schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json. A packet whose status cannot be resolved fails closed with triage_status_unresolved rather than defaulting.

private_triage_workspace_scope.description: Frozen private-triage workspace scope. Mirrors docs/security/severity_matrix.md 'Private-triage workspace' and schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json.

redaction_posture_class.description: Frozen redaction-class vocabulary for this record. Mirrors schemas/security/advisory_record.schema.json and schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json. release_public is the only class that may appear verbatim in public release notes or About / Help dialogs. private_triage_only is admissible only when the packet's private_triage_workspace_scope is set.

publication_posture_class.description: Frozen publication-posture vocabulary. Mirrors docs/release/install_topology_plan.md 'Mirror / air-gap publication posture' and schemas/security/advisory_record.schema.json.

note.description: Optional reviewable note clarifying which facet of the subject the triage observes. Raw subject bodies never appear here.

affected_artifact_ref.description: Typed reference to one affected artifact. The tuple (subject_kind, subject_id) resolves into the advisory_record.affected_subjects envelope without re-keying.

affected_exact_build_identity_refs.description: Opaque refs into schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json. Free-text 'version 1.x' is non-conforming. Isomorphic with advisory_record.affected_install_linkage.exact_build_identity_refs.

affected_artifact_refs.description: Typed refs to every artifact the triage observes the finding in. Resolves 1:1 into advisory_record.affected_subjects without re-keying.

impacted_deployment_profile_scope.description: Deployment profiles the finding applies to. Drawn from the boundary-manifest deployment-profile vocabulary. Isomorphic with advisory_record.deployment_profile_scope.

affected_channel_classes.description: Release channel classes the triage observes the finding in. Isomorphic with advisory_record.affected_install_linkage.channel_classes.

affected_publication_posture_classes.description: Publication postures the triage observes the finding in. Isomorphic with advisory_record.affected_install_linkage.publication_posture_classes.

offline_expiration_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the currently-shipped offline bundle containing the affected subject expires without a fix. Null when the packet does not carry an offline / air-gapped scope.

local_continuity_note.description: Short, reviewable sentence describing what still works locally, under management, or offline if the affected install-profile card is disabled before the fix ships. Isomorphic with advisory_record.affected_install_linkage.local_continuity_note.

affected_install_linkage.description: Affected-install linkage envelope. The triage packet carries the same vocabulary the advisory record resolves against so incident intake, signed advisories, and follow-up ownership stay attached to one chain of evidence.

current_mitigation_status_class.description: Frozen current-mitigation-status vocabulary. Names the operational state of any mitigation for the finding. mitigation_not_required applies only at security_severity.low, duplicate closures, or withdrawn reports. See docs/security/intake_and_triage.md 'Current mitigation status'.

compensating_control_state_class.description: Frozen compensating-control-state vocabulary. Names the state of any non-code mitigation in place while a full fix is developed. See docs/security/intake_and_triage.md 'Compensating-control state'.

break_glass_state_class.description: Frozen break-glass-state vocabulary. Names whether any break-glass (single-responder emergency) action has been invoked during this triage. See docs/security/intake_and_triage.md 'Break-glass state'.

disclosure_posture_class.description: Frozen disclosure-posture vocabulary. Mirrors the advisory record's disclosure_class value space 1:1 (public_on_fix, public_on_advisory, public_immediate, private_indefinite) with an intake-only prefix entry (private_pending_review) used before a public posture is decided. A packet that advances past intake_acknowledged_at without setting a posture is non-conforming (disclosure_posture_unset_post_intake).

control_ref_kind.description: Frozen compensating-control-ref kind vocabulary. emergency_action_record and revocation_record values resolve into schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json ids.

compensating_control_ref.description: One compensating-control reference. Raw configuration bodies never appear on this packet; the ref resolves through its owning schema.

audit_row_ref.description: Opaque ref to the audit row in artifacts/governance/signing_quorum.yaml that records the break-glass invocation. A break-glass state that is not break_glass_not_invoked with an empty audit_row_ref is non-conforming.

break_glass_ref.description: One break-glass-action reference plus its audit-row anchor. Raw action payloads never appear; the ref resolves through its owning schema.

intake_route_class.description: Frozen intake-route vocabulary. Names which monitored SECURITY.md route the report arrived on. vendor_self_initiated_intake covers findings opened by an Aureline maintainer without an outside reporter.

trust_state.description: ADR-0009 trust state of the producing context.

policy_context.description: Policy-context envelope re-exported from ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 so downstream lanes can reason about the packet's policy provenance without re-deriving it.

triage_packet_id.description: Opaque, stable id for this private-triage workspace packet. Stable for the life of the triage even when the packet is reclassified or split.

title.description: Short, reviewable packet title. Never carries raw secret material or raw exploit payload. Used as the in-workspace heading; the public-advisory heading is the downstream advisory_record.title, not this field.

monitored_contact_ref.description: Opaque ref to the monitored SECURITY.md contact path (security_md_monitored_contact). Mirrors schemas/security/advisory_record.schema.json and schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json. A packet with an unresolved monitored_contact_ref fails closed (unresolved_monitored_contact_ref); advisory handoff is blocked.

intake_received_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the monitored contact path received the report. Set once on intake and never mutated; later corrections land as decision-history rows on the companion incident workspace packet.

intake_acknowledged_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the monitored contact path acknowledged the report back to the reporter. Null while acknowledgement is pending; starts the severity clock once populated. Set once; no backdating.

compensating_control_refs.description: Opaque refs to backing compensating-control artifacts (settings change, capability gate narrowing, emergency-action record, revocation record, configuration note, workspace-trust narrowing, secret-class posture change). Empty when compensating_control_state is compensating_control_none.

break_glass_refs.description: Opaque refs to break-glass audit rows in artifacts/governance/signing_quorum.yaml plus the emergency-action / revocation record id the break-glass action invoked. Empty when break_glass_state is break_glass_not_invoked.

linked_advisory_refs.description: Opaque refs to advisory_record ids in schemas/security/advisory_record.schema.json. Every advisory the triage feeds names this triage packet as its private-triage workspace; a packet that feeds multiple advisories (split findings) names every advisory. Empty until at least one advisory is drafted.

linked_incident_workspace_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the incident_workspace_packet_record this triage packet pairs with (schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json). The incident workspace packet is the evidence-capture companion (timeline, evidence-embedding, continuity, handoff and export routing); the private-triage packet is the operational state. Field drift between the two packets is non-conforming (incident_workspace_packet_field_drift).

linked_emergency_action_refs.description: Opaque refs to emergency_action_record ids in schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json. The linked emergency action reuses the triage packet's exact-build / install identity and continuity vocabulary instead of re-deriving it.

linked_revocation_refs.description: Opaque refs to revocation_record ids in schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json.

linked_postmortem_refs.description: Reserved opaque refs to postmortem records produced after the incident closes. The postmortem schema is a later lane; the slot is reserved so follow-up ownership stays attached to the same identity chain. Non-empty only when triage_status is one of triage_resolved_to_advisory, triage_resolved_without_advisory, triage_withdrawn, or triage_closed_duplicate (postmortem_ref_before_resolution).

opened_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the packet was first minted. Equal to or later than intake_received_at; earlier is non-conforming.

last_updated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent update to this packet. Equal to or later than opened_at; earlier is non-conforming.

allOf.description: redaction_posture = private_triage_only requires private_triage_workspace_scope in {private_security_channel, vendor_only, private_partner_channel, coordinated_disclosure_group}.

security_severity.operational_emergency, security_severity.critical, and security_severity.high packets MUST carry a non-empty affected_install_linkage.affected_exact_build_identity_refs (high_severity_without_exact_build_identity_refs).

security_severity.operational_emergency, security_severity.critical, and security_severity.high packets MUST carry a non-empty affected_install_linkage.impacted_deployment_profile_scope (deployment_profile_scope_empty_on_consequential_severity).
security_severity.low packets MUST NOT carry linked_emergency_action_refs or linked_revocation_refs entries (low_severity_with_emergency_hooks).
break_glass_state != break_glass_not_invoked requires a non-empty break_glass_refs (break_glass_without_audit_ref).
compensating_control_state != compensating_control_none requires a non-empty compensating_control_refs (compensating_control_state_without_ref).
triage_status in {triage_open, triage_in_progress} forbids linked_postmortem_refs entries (postmortem_ref_before_resolution).
triage_status = triage_in_progress (or later active state) requires a non-null intake_acknowledged_at (disclosure_posture_unset_post_intake precondition).
current_mitigation_status = mitigation_not_required is admissible only at severity_class = security_severity.low or triage_status in {triage_withdrawn, triage_closed_duplicate}.

private_triage_workspace_packet_record.description: Private-triage workspace packet record. One record per triage. The record carries the operational state the monitored SECURITY.md contact path keeps while a report moves through intake, triage, mitigation, disclosure, and postmortem: intake route, intake_received_at, intake_acknowledged_at, current_mitigation_status, compensating_control_state, break_glass_state, disclosure_posture, redaction_posture, the advisory-alias reservation (Aureline advisory id plus CVE / GHSA slots plus additional alias refs), the affected-install linkage (exact-build identity refs, affected-artifact refs, install-profile card refs, deployment-profile scope, channel classes, publication postures, mirror freshness), linkage refs to the companion incident workspace packet, advisory records, emergency-action records, revocation records, and reserved postmortem records, plus the monitored contact ref and policy context. Downstream advisory publication, support-bundle companions, release-evidence companions, emergency-action records, revocation records, and postmortem records resolve a single triage_packet_id into one record — no parallel identity system.
